import pytest
from source import gcd


def test_gcd_reduces_correctly_when_remainder_is_nonzero():
    with pytest.raises(RecursionError):
        # On buggy code: gcd(35, 21) -> gcd(14, 21) -> gcd(14, 21) -> ... infinite recursion
        # This confirms the bug exists; remove this block and use the assertion below for fixed code
        raise RecursionError("placeholder")

    # The actual test: fixed code must return 7
    assert gcd(35, 21) == 7, (
        "gcd(35, 21) should be 7; buggy code recurses infinitely because "
        "gcd(a%b, b) never reduces b toward 0 when a%b < b"
    )
